summary: Iteration 45: Tested Google AdSense integration in blog posts. All features working correctly - AdSenseUnit component renders, admin blog editor has AdSense tab with code input and position selector, saving blog posts with AdSense code works, and the AdSense container appears in blog posts when adsense_code is set.
passed_tests: Blog API health check - returns list of posts
Create blog post with AdSense code - saves adsense_code and adsense_position fields
Create blog posts with different AdSense positions (before_content, after_first_paragraph, middle_content, after_content)
Update existing blog post with AdSense code
Blog posts without AdSense still work correctly
Get blog post by slug returns correct data including adsense fields
New Post dialog has Content and AdSense tabs
AdSense tab has code textarea with placeholder example
AdSense tab has position selector dropdown
AdSense tab has instructions on how to get AdSense code
Saving blog post with AdSense code shows success toast
Blog post list shows code icon (<>) for posts with AdSense
Blog post page loads AdSenseUnit component with data-testid='adsense-unit'
adsbygoogle ins element is created and push() is called
critical_code_review_comments: AdSenseUnit.jsx correctly parses AdSense code to extract data-ad-client and data-ad-slot attributes
Component properly loads Google AdSense script dynamically
Creates proper ins elements with all required attributes
Has proper error handling with silent fail for end users
BlogPostPage.jsx correctly uses AdSenseUnit component based on adsense_position
BlogContentWithAds component properly splits content and inserts ad at correct position
AdminBlog.jsx has well-designed AdSense tab with clear instructions
seed_data_creation: Created test blog post 'UI_TEST_AdSense_Blog_Post' with AdSense code. Added AdSense code to 'Website as a Service' post for testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 45 verified the AdSense integration feature. The AdSenseUnit component properly handles AdSense code parsing, script loading, and ad initialization. Note: Actual ads won't display without a valid Google AdSense account and approved domain - this is expected behavior. The component shows 'Script error' in console when using invalid publisher IDs, which is normal. Test posts with AdSense code were created for verification.
notes: The 'Script error' shown on the blog post page is expected behavior when using test/invalid AdSense publisher IDs. Google's AdSense server returns 400 error for invalid accounts. The AdSenseUnit component is working correctly - it loads the script, creates the ins element, and calls push(). Real ads will only display with a valid AdSense account and approved domain.
